response.text:
# REFLECTION

r1c2 is in critical condition: islanded with SoC at 0.37/4 kWh (9.25%), consuming 1.00 kW, and facing systematic rejection from all peers (r0c2, r1c1, r2c2). The cluster-wide stress is real—every neighbor is in conservation mode, and r2c2 has explicitly distrusted r1c2. Inbound requests from r0c2 and r1c3 remain unmet. The previous policy's escalation to "urgent" has failed to unlock supply; peers cite their own islanded states and declining reserves as justification. At current draw rate, r1c2 has ~22 minutes of autonomy before SoC reaches critical depletion. The pattern of repeated rejections with identical reasoning suggests the cluster is in a deadlock: all nodes are simultaneously protecting their own reserves. Continuing to request 0.5 kWh at urgent priority is unlikely to succeed. A shift toward accepting smaller inbound offers, reducing outbound demand, and signaling willingness to accept any available supply—even fractional amounts—may be necessary to break the deadlock and survive the islanded period.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.15
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: [r2c2]
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cluster deadlock detected—all peers rejecting due to own reserve depletion. r1c2 SoC critically low (0.37/4 kWh). Shift to accepting fractional inbound supply; suspend outbound sharing entirely. Escalate request frequency and accept lower thresholds to survive islanded operation."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
